title: Behind the scenes with Best Actor nominees at 2026 Academy Awards - CBS News
text: The 98th Academy Awards featured five best actor nominees, each bringing distinct performances to their roles. Timothée Chalamet (Marty Supreme) plays an ambitious table tennis hustler in 1950s New York. At 30, he became the youngest male to earn three best actor nominations, surpassing Marlon Brando's previous record. Leonardo DiCaprio (One Battle After Another) portrays Bob, a lapsed revolutionary hiding in California. Inspired by Jeff Bridges' approach in The Big Lebowski, DiCaprio earned his eighth Oscar nomination for this Paul Thomas Anderson film. Ethan Hawke (Blue Moon) tackled the role of lyricist Lorenz Hart in Richard Linklater's period piece. The production took about a decade to develop, during which Linklater and Hawke workshopped the script extensively. Michael B. Jordan (Sinners) played dual roles as vampire-fighting twins in Ryan Coogler's supernatural drama. He won the SAG Award and went on to win the Oscar. Accepting the award, he stated: Thank you for keep betting on me. And I'm going to keep stepping up. Wagner Moura (The Secret Agent) earned the best actor prize at Cannes for his performance in this Brazilian political thriller set during the late 1970s dictatorship. He plays a mysterious government functionary harboring dangerous secrets.
